sanskrit:
न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम्।
जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान् युक्तः समाचरन्॥
transliteration: na buddhi-bhedaṃ janayed ajñānāṃ karma-saṅginām | joṣayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācaran ||
literal_meaning: Let not the wise create confusion in the intelligence of the ignorant who are attached to action; rather, the enlightened, remaining established in yoga, should encourage all to perform their duties by himself doing them properly.
contextual_meaning: A psychologically mature person does not abruptly dismantle another’s coping structure (action-based identity) but models a higher integration while validating the other’s current stage, preventing cognitive dissonance and defensive regression.
psychological_state: Secure-attachment stance: the wise retain ego-stability while holding space for the other’s growth, avoiding superiority-induced shame in the less integrated.
trigger_scenario: A senior developer sees juniors grinding overtime to prove worth. Instead of preaching "work-life balance,” she pairs with them, quietly demonstrating efficient code and healthy boundaries, letting them internalize the upgrade without feeling judged.
anchor_text: Model calm duty; don’t yank others from their work.
